input: Write landing page hero copy for Glint, a B2B SaaS tool that auto-generates weekly status reports from Jira + Slack. Target persona: engineering managers at 50-200 person startups who spend 3+ hours/week manually writing updates for execs. They're frustrated, not lazy — they just want the time back. Tone: direct, no fluff. Deliver 3 hero headline variants + 1 subheadline each.

Write 3 Facebook ad copy variants for Ember Candles, a DTC e-commerce brand. Product: hand-poured soy candles, $38–$58, 'designed for people who take their home seriously.' Audience: women 28–44, homeowners, follow interior design accounts, spend on Etsy and CB2. The ad will run in the feed. No stock-photo vibes. Speak to taste, not discount. Max 125 chars per ad body.

Write 5 email subject lines for a cold outreach campaign. Product: Loom alternative called Clipr — async video messaging for remote sales teams, free tier up to 10 videos/mo. Target: VP Sales at SaaS companies 100–500 employees. Goal: get a demo booked. Subject lines must be under 50 characters. No question marks (internal A/B test rule). No emojis.

Write a B2B pricing page value stack for Vaultly, a document automation platform. Plans: Starter $49/mo, Growth $149/mo, Enterprise custom. The Starter→Growth upgrade conversion is the priority. Growth includes unlimited templates, team collaboration, and Salesforce sync. Typical buyer: RevOps managers. They care about time-to-value, not features. Surface the ROI angle. 3 distinct framings of the Growth plan.

Write an abandoned cart email for a Shopify store selling $220 mechanical keyboards (niche enthusiast market). Cart was abandoned 2 hours ago. Subscriber is opted-in. No discount — the brand never discounts. Angle: remind them why they wanted it. Subject line + email body (max 150 words). Speak to craftsmanship and the feeling of a good keyboard. Not 'you forgot something.'

Write homepage hero copy for Fiora, a consumer app for tracking personal habits and mood. Target: adults 25–40 dealing with burnout who've tried and quit 3+ habit apps. They're skeptical of gamification and streaks. Differentiator: Fiora uses 'gentle accountability' — no streaks, no notifications unless you ask, no shame spirals. 3 hero headline + sub-headline pairs.

Write 3 Google search ad headlines (max 30 chars each) and 2 descriptions (max 90 chars each) for an online accounting software targeting freelancers. Competitor: FreshBooks. Positioning: 'designed for one-person businesses, not agencies.' USPs: auto-categorizes bank imports, sends invoices in 2 clicks, $9/mo. Bid on the keyword 'freelance accounting software'.

Write 3 variants of an upsell popup for a SaaS analytics tool. Context: user is on the free plan, just viewed their 3rd report this week (limit is 5/week). The upsell target is the $29/mo Pro plan which includes unlimited reports + CSV export. Copy must feel helpful, not pushy. Max 2 sentences per variant. Include a CTA button label for each.

Write a product launch announcement email for Draftly, an AI writing assistant built specifically for LinkedIn content creators. Launch is going to 8,000 subscribers who signed up on a waitlist. They've been waiting 4 months. Don't open with 'We're excited to announce.' Build anticipation in the first 2 lines, deliver the news, include 3 bullets on what's new, end with a single CTA.

Write copy for a retargeting ad on LinkedIn targeting people who visited the Solaris ERP pricing page but didn't convert. Solaris is enterprise ERP for mid-market manufacturers ($500K–$5M ACV deals). These visitors saw pricing and left — likely sticker shock or needing internal buy-in. The ad should re-engage with a softer CTA (ROI calculator, not demo). 3 ad variants, 150 chars max body copy each.
